steps: Find the total volume of the cylindrical can using \(V = \text{base area} \times \text{height} = 75 \times 10 = 750\) cm\(^3\).

The can is filled with fruit pieces and then syrup is poured in to fill it completely to the top. The syrup occupies the space not taken by fruit: \(V_{\text{fruit}} = V_{\text{can}} - V_{\text{syrup}} = 750 - 110 = 640\) cm\(^3\).

Evaluate each answer choice:
  • Choice A: 7.5 cm\(^3\) — this would result from dividing 75 by 10, not using the correct formula. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: 185 cm\(^3\) — this would result from adding 75 and 110, which has no physical meaning here. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: 640 cm\(^3\) — equals \(750 - 110\), the total can volume minus the syrup. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • Choice D: 750 cm\(^3\) — this is the total can volume, not the fruit volume alone. Eliminated.
